questions.question: Who said, 'There shall be neither dew nor rain these years except by my word'?
questions.explanation: Elijah the Tishbite declared this to King Ahab as a judgment from God against Israel's idolatry under Ahab and Jezebel's rule.
questions.hint: This prophet confronted 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel.

questions.question: In the case before Solomon over the dead and the living child, what did the mother of the dead child support?
options: Giving the living child to her opponent
Joint ownership of the living child
King Solomon having the living child
questions.explanation: The false mother, whose child had died, agreed to the division of the living child, revealing her lack of true maternal love.
questions.hint: Solomon's wisdom was revealed when he suggested dividing the child.

questions.question: Before Moses died, who did God appoint to succeed him?
questions.explanation: God appointed Joshua son of Nun to succeed Moses and lead the Israelites into the Promised Land.
questions.hint: This leader led the Israelites across the Jordan River into Canaan.

questions.question: What was the treatment for those bitten by the fiery serpent in the wilderness?
questions.explanation: God instructed Moses to make a bronze serpent on a pole; anyone bitten who looked at it would live.
questions.hint: Jesus referenced this event when speaking to Nicodemus.

questions.question: In order that Benjamin might be brought to Egypt, which of Joseph's brothers did Joseph detain?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Joseph and His Brothers
questions.explanation: Joseph detained Simeon and bound him while his brothers returned to Canaan to bring Benjamin.
questions.hint: This brother was the second son of Jacob by Leah.

questions.question: When Joseph's brothers came to him in Egypt, what accusation did he make against them?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Joseph and His Brothers
questions.explanation: Joseph accused his brothers of being spies who came to see the nakedness of the land, as a test to uncover their character.
questions.hint: This accusation was part of Joseph's plan to test his brothers' honesty.

questions.question: Who did Sarah give birth to in her old age?
questions.explanation: Sarah, though barren and advanced in years, gave birth to Isaac as God had promised Abraham.
questions.hint: His name means 'laughter' because Sarah laughed when God promised him.

questions.question: As a condition of his covenant with God, what did Abraham have to do?
questions.explanation: God commanded Abraham to circumcise every male among his household as a sign of the everlasting covenant between God and Abraham's descendants.
questions.hint: This sign was given when Abraham was 99 years old.

questions.question: What was the condition of the earth before the creation of the world?
options: The earth was without form and void
There was a firmament above the earth
The heaven was very close to the earth
questions.explanation: Genesis 1:2 states that the earth was without form and void, and darkness was upon the face of the deep.
questions.hint: The Spirit of God was moving over the face of the waters.

questions.question: Why did God want to destroy the world in the days of Noah?
options: The sons of God took daughters of men as wives
The earth was filled with violence
questions.topic: Old Testament - Noah and the Flood
questions.explanation: Genesis 6:11-13 states that the earth was corrupt and filled with violence, so God decided to destroy all flesh.
questions.hint: Only Noah found favor in God's eyes.

questions.question: What did Adonijah do to show that he feared Solomon?
options: Caught hold of the horns of the altar
questions.topic: Old Testament - Solomon's Rise to Power
questions.explanation: When Adonijah heard that Solomon had been made king, he feared for his life and went to catch hold of the horns of the altar for refuge.
questions.hint: The horns of the altar were considered a place of asylum.

questions.question: Where did Solomon get the timber for his building projects?
questions.explanation: Solomon obtained cedar and cypress timber from Lebanon through a treaty with Hiram, king of Tyre.
questions.hint: The cedars of this place were famous throughout the ancient world.

questions.question: According to Jeremiah's prophecy, the domination of nations by Nebuchadnezzar was what?
options: The result of his military might
The result of the wickedness of nations
questions.explanation: Jeremiah prophesied that Nebuchadnezzar was God's servant, sent to bring judgment upon nations for their sins, showing God's sovereignty.
questions.hint: God used foreign kings to accomplish His purposes.

questions.question: What was Ezekiel ordered to eat before going to speak to the house of Israel?
questions.explanation: God commanded Ezekiel to eat the scroll containing words of lamentation and woe, symbolizing internalizing God's message before delivering it.
questions.hint: The scroll tasted as sweet as honey in his mouth.

questions.question: Where was Abraham asked to sacrifice Isaac?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Abraham's Test of Faith
questions.explanation: God commanded Abraham to go to the land of Moriah and offer Isaac as a burnt offering on one of the mountains there.
questions.hint: This is the same location where Solomon later built the Temple.

questions.question: How was the wife of Isaac identified as the one chosen by God?
options: By her coming to the well to draw water
Because she was a descendant of Abraham's brother
Because she said 'Drink, and I will water your camels'
questions.explanation: Abraham's servant prayed for a sign: the woman who offered water to him and his camels would be Isaac's wife. Rebekah fulfilled this sign.
questions.hint: This act of kindness demonstrated her character and hospitality.

questions.question: Who was Achan in the Old Testament?
options: A spy who visited Rahab in Jericho
One who prophesied in the camp
One of the judges of Israel
The one who took devoted things when Jericho fell
questions.explanation: Achan took devoted items from Jericho, causing Israel to be defeated at Ai. He and his household were stoned as punishment.
questions.hint: His sin caused the death of 36 Israelite soldiers.

questions.question: Who said, 'How is it you were not afraid to put forth your hand to destroy the Lord's anointed?'
questions.explanation: After Saul's death, an Amalekite claimed to have killed Saul. David responded by executing him for striking down the Lord's anointed.
questions.hint: David had previously refused to harm Saul when he had the opportunity.

questions.question: Which prophet was regularly addressed as 'son of man'?
questions.explanation: God addressed Ezekiel as 'son of man' over 90 times in the book of Ezekiel, emphasizing his humanity as God's messenger.
questions.hint: This title was also used for Jesus in the New Testament.

questions.question: What was the last miracle God performed before the Israelites were released from Egypt?
questions.explanation: The death of the firstborn in Egypt was the tenth and final plague, which finally convinced Pharaoh to release the Israelites.
questions.hint: The Passover was instituted to protect the Israelites from this plague.

questions.question: Who proved on Mount Carmel that Yahweh is greater than Baal?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Elijah on Mount Carmel
questions.explanation: Elijah challenged 450 prophets of Baal to a contest; God answered by fire from heaven, proving He is the true God.
questions.hint: Elijah had water poured around the altar three times before God answered.

questions.question: What did God make on the third day of creation?
questions.explanation: On the third day, God gathered the waters to create dry land (earth) and caused vegetation to spring forth.
questions.hint: This was before the sun, moon, and stars were created.

questions.question: When God told Moses to cut two tablets of stone like the first, on what occasion was this said?
options: At the renewal of the Covenant
Just before the election of Joshua
After the first fall of Manna
questions.topic: Old Testament - Renewal of the Covenant
questions.explanation: After Moses broke the first tablets due to Israel's golden calf sin, God commanded him to cut new tablets for the renewal of the covenant.
questions.hint: Moses spent another 40 days on Mount Sinai.

questions.question: How was the city of Jericho captured by the Israelites?
options: After a fierce battle lasting seven days
Because the defence system was weak
At the blast of the trumpets of the priests
questions.explanation: The Israelites marched around Jericho for seven days; on the seventh day, the priests blew trumpets, the people shouted, and the walls fell down.
questions.hint: Rahab and her family were spared.

questions.question: When did the call of Isaiah take place?
options: In the year of King Uzziah's death
questions.explanation: Isaiah saw the Lord in the year that King Uzziah died and received his prophetic call to go to the people of Israel.
questions.hint: He saw seraphim crying 'Holy, holy, holy'.

questions.question: What happened when Zechariah doubted the announcement of the birth of John the Baptist?
questions.topic: New Testament - Birth of John the Baptist
questions.explanation: Because Zechariah doubted Gabriel's message that his elderly wife Elizabeth would bear a son, he was made unable to speak until John's birth.
questions.hint: His speech returned when he named the child John.

questions.question: Why did Herod want to kill the child Jesus?
options: He thought he would become a prophet
He learnt he was born King of the Jews
He was born of a virgin
questions.explanation: Herod was threatened when the wise men asked where the King of the Jews had been born, fearing a rival to his throne.
questions.hint: Joseph was warned in a dream to flee to Egypt.

questions.question: What is the main lesson of the parable of the prodigal son?
options: A son should always obey his father
God forgives those who sincerely repent
questions.explanation: The parable teaches God's abundant grace and forgiveness for repentant sinners, as the father joyfully welcomed his wayward son home.
questions.hint: The older brother represents those who resent God's mercy.

questions.question: 'These men who have turned the world upside down have come here also, and Jason has received them.' Which town is referred to?
questions.explanation: This accusation was made against Paul and Silas in Thessalonica, where Jason hosted them and was brought before the city authorities.
questions.hint: Paul wrote two epistles to the church in this city.

questions.question: 'And you, child, will be called the prophet of the Most High' refers to whom?
questions.topic: New Testament - Birth of John the Baptist
questions.explanation: Zechariah, filled with the Holy Spirit, prophesied that his son John would be called the prophet of the Most High, preparing the way for the Lord.
questions.hint: John was the forerunner of Jesus Christ.

questions.question: What does the parable of the mustard seed teach?
options: The kingdom of God is invisible
The kingdom will expand from small beginnings
The coming of the kingdom is uncertain
questions.explanation: The mustard seed, though smallest of seeds, grows into a large plant, illustrating how God's kingdom grows from humble beginnings to worldwide impact.
questions.hint: Birds nest in its branches.

questions.question: When the women got to the tomb of Jesus, what did they find?
options: Jesus coming out of the tomb
Jesus was no longer in the tomb
questions.explanation: The women found the stone rolled away and the tomb empty; Jesus had risen from the dead as He had promised.
questions.hint: An angel told them He had risen.

questions.question: Complete the quote: 'You shall be my witnesses in Jerusalem and in all Judea and ... and to the end of the earth.'
questions.explanation: Jesus commanded His disciples to be witnesses starting from Jerusalem, then Judea, Samaria, and to the ends of the earth, showing the global scope of the gospel.
questions.hint: This order is fulfilled in the book of Acts.

questions.question: Why was Paul first arrested in Jerusalem?
options: He brought Greeks into the temple
He preached Christ in the temple
questions.explanation: Jews from Asia accused Paul of bringing Greeks into the temple area, defiling the holy place, which led to his arrest.
questions.hint: They had previously seen him with Trophimus the Ephesian.

questions.question: Which country in Africa provided Jesus with a refuge when Herod sought to destroy him?
questions.explanation: An angel told Joseph to flee to Egypt with Mary and Jesus to escape Herod's massacre, fulfilling the prophecy, 'Out of Egypt I called my son.'
questions.hint: They remained there until Herod's death.

questions.question: Who said, 'After me comes he who is mightier than I, the thong of whose sandals I am not worthy to stoop down and untie'?
questions.explanation: John the Baptist spoke these words about Jesus, expressing his humility and recognizing Jesus' superiority over him.
questions.hint: He also said 'He must increase, but I must decrease.'

questions.question: Who addressed Jesus at Jericho saying, 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me'?
questions.explanation: Blind Bartimaeus cried out to Jesus for mercy, recognizing Him as the Messiah ('Son of David'), and received his sight.
questions.hint: He threw off his cloak and sprang up to come to Jesus.

questions.question: By whom did the Lord heal the blindness of Paul?
questions.explanation: After Paul was struck blind on the road to Damascus, the Lord sent Ananias to lay hands on him, restoring his sight.
questions.hint: Ananias was initially afraid because of Saul's reputation.

questions.question: Who insisted that God does not dwell in houses made with hands and that it was wrong for Solomon to have built a house for Him?
questions.explanation: Stephen, in his speech before the Sanhedrin, declared that the Most High does not dwell in houses made with hands, referencing Solomon's temple.
questions.hint: This speech led to his martyrdom.

questions.question: 'The Holy Spirit will come upon you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you; therefore the child to be born will be called holy, the Son of God.' To whom was this said?
questions.explanation: The angel Gabriel spoke these words to Mary, announcing that she would conceive Jesus by the Holy Spirit, even though she was a virgin.
questions.hint: Mary responded, 'Let it be to me according to your word.'

questions.question: Where did Jesus say, 'But I say to you that hear, love your enemies, do good to those who hate you, bless those who curse you, pray for those who abuse you'?
options: During the Sermon on the Mount
questions.topic: New Testament - Sermon on the Mount
questions.explanation: This teaching on loving enemies is part of Jesus' Sermon on the Mount (Matthew) or Sermon on the Plain (Luke), emphasizing radical Christian love.
questions.hint: This contrasts with 'an eye for an eye' teaching.

questions.question: When complaints were made that some sections of the new Christian community were not being supplied with their needs, what did the Apostles do?
options: Said they had neither gold nor silver
questions.topic: New Testament - Early Church (Choosing of Deacons)
questions.explanation: The apostles appointed seven men of good repute, full of the Spirit and wisdom, to oversee the daily distribution of food to the Hellenistic widows.
questions.hint: Stephen and Philip were among the seven.

questions.question: When Paul came to Rome, who were the first set of people to whom he preached?
questions.explanation: Paul's custom was to preach to the Jews first; in Rome, he called together the local Jewish leaders to explain his situation and preach the gospel.
questions.hint: Some believed, but others did not.

questions.question: 'Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb.' Who said this?
questions.topic: New Testament - Visitation of Mary to Elizabeth
questions.explanation: Elizabeth, filled with the Holy Spirit, spoke these words to Mary when Mary came to visit her, recognizing her as the mother of the Lord.
questions.hint: John the Baptist leaped in Elizabeth's womb at Mary's greeting.

questions.question: Who led Jesus into the wilderness?
questions.explanation: The Holy Spirit led Jesus into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil, showing that Jesus' temptation was part of God's divine plan.
questions.hint: Jesus fasted for 40 days and 40 nights.

questions.question: Complete the quote: 'And he came to ... where he had been brought up; and, as his custom was, he went into the synagogue on the Sabbath Day, and stood up to read.'
questions.explanation: Jesus returned to Nazareth, where He was raised, and read from Isaiah in the synagogue, declaring the prophecy fulfilled in their hearing.
questions.hint: The people rejected Him and tried to throw Him off a cliff.

questions.question: 'Therefore in the resurrection whose wife shall she be of the seven for they all had her?' Who put this question to Jesus?
questions.explanation: The Sadducees, who did not believe in the resurrection, asked Jesus this question to mock the concept of resurrection, but Jesus corrected their misunderstanding.
questions.hint: Jesus told them they know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God.

questions.question: Why did Pilate give Jesus to the Jews to be crucified?
options: The Jews brought pressure upon him
questions.explanation: Pilate found no fault in Jesus but yielded to the pressure of the Jewish leaders and the crowd, who threatened to report him to Caesar.
questions.hint: Pilate washed his hands to symbolize his innocence.

questions.question: Why was Paul converted?
options: To help Peter in the work
Not to be killed for his sins
To bear the name of the Lord before the Gentiles
questions.explanation: Jesus told Ananias that Paul was a chosen instrument to carry His name before Gentiles, kings, and the children of Israel.
questions.hint: Paul would also suffer for the name of Jesus.

questions.question: Where were Paul and Silas imprisoned?
questions.topic: New Testament - Paul and Silas in Prison
questions.explanation: In Philippi, Paul and Silas were beaten and imprisoned after casting a spirit of divination out of a slave girl, but at midnight they prayed and sang, and an earthquake freed them.
questions.hint: The Philippian jailer and his household were converted that night.

questions.question: After the Flood, God allowed all food to be eaten except what?
options: Flesh with its blood in it
questions.explanation: God gave Noah and his descendants every moving thing that lives for food, but strictly forbade eating flesh with its lifeblood still in it, as blood represents life.
questions.hint: This command predates the Mosaic dietary laws.

questions.question: 'Two nations are in your womb, and two peoples born of you shall be divided...' To whom was this addressed by God?
questions.explanation: God revealed to Rebekah that she would have twins who would become two nations: the older (Esau/Edom) would serve the younger (Jacob/Israel).
questions.hint: This prophecy was fulfilled throughout Old Testament history.

questions.question: Who was the wife of Moses?
questions.explanation: Moses married Zipporah, the daughter of Jethro (also called Reuel), the priest of Midian, while he was in exile from Egypt.
questions.hint: She circumcised their son to save Moses' life on the way to Egypt.

questions.question: '... I the Lord your God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children to the third and fourth generation...' When was this statement made?
options: When God gave the Ten Commandments to Moses
When Aaron's family was elected to priesthood
When God commanded Moses to go to Pharaoh
questions.explanation: This statement is part of the Second Commandment (or first, depending on numbering) delivered to Moses on Mount Sinai, emphasizing God's jealousy against idolatry.
questions.hint: This demonstrates God's holiness and the serious consequences of sin.

questions.question: Who were the three sons of Noah?
questions.explanation: Noah's three sons were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. From them, the whole earth was populated after the flood.
questions.hint: Shem is the ancestor of Abraham and the Semitic peoples.

questions.question: For rebelling against Moses and God, the Israelites were bitten by fiery snakes. What was the cure for the snakebites?
options: A drink of water from the rock
Anointing the affected part with oil
To call on the name of Yahweh
A look at the bronze serpent
questions.explanation: God instructed Moses to make a bronze serpent and put it on a pole; anyone who was bitten and looked at it would live, demonstrating salvation by faith.
questions.hint: Jesus compared Himself to this bronze serpent in John 3:14-15.

questions.question: Why was Joshua appointed to lead the Israelites to the Promised Land?
options: Moses was too old to lead them
Moses never returned from Mount Sinai
Because of Moses' rebellion in the wilderness of Zin
questions.explanation: Moses was not allowed to enter the Promised Land because he struck the rock at Meribah instead of speaking to it as God commanded, so God appointed Joshua.
questions.hint: Moses saw the Promised Land from Mount Nebo but did not enter.

questions.question: Why was the covenant renewed on Mount Sinai?
options: A new generation did not remember the first
The people took part in Korah's rebellion
The people demanded water from the rock
Aaron had made a golden calf
questions.explanation: While Moses was on Mount Sinai receiving the Ten Commandments, the Israelites made a golden calf to worship. Moses broke the first tablets, and God renewed the covenant after judgment.
questions.hint: About 3,000 people died because of the golden calf incident.

questions.question: Those inspired by God to lead the tribes of Israel in battle were the?
questions.explanation: God raised up judges (such as Deborah, Gideon, Samson, and Jephthah) to deliver Israel from their oppressors and lead them in battle during the period before kings.
questions.hint: The Book of Judges records the cycles of sin, oppression, repentance, and deliverance.

questions.question: Each of the spies sent out by Moses on God's command to report on the nature of the Promised Land was a?
questions.explanation: Moses sent twelve spies, one from each tribe, each a leader or prince among his people, to scout the land of Canaan for forty days.
questions.hint: Only Joshua and Caleb brought back a favorable report.

questions.question: Why did God reveal to Samuel that He was going to punish Eli?
options: Eli did not correct his sons' sinful behavior
Eli did not offer sacrifice as the law required
Eli did not warn the people of Israel
Eli warned his sons but did not punish them
questions.topic: Old Testament - Eli and His Sons
questions.explanation: Eli's sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were wicked priests who treated God's offerings with contempt, and Eli failed to restrain them despite knowing their sins.
questions.hint: The prophecy against Eli's house was fulfilled when his sons died on the same day.

questions.question: When did the sun stand still?
options: When Moses led Israel out of Egypt
When Noah came out of the ark
When Joshua defeated the Amorites at Gibeon
questions.explanation: Joshua prayed for the sun and moon to stand still so Israel could defeat the Amorites at Gibeon, and God miraculously extended the daylight until victory was complete.
questions.hint: There has never been a day like it before or since.

questions.question: 'I repent that I made Saul king.' What incident does this refer to?
options: During Saul's encounter with the Ammonites
When Saul disobeyed God over Amalek
questions.explanation: God rejected Saul as king because he disobeyed the command to utterly destroy the Amalekites and their livestock, sparing King Agag and the best animals.
questions.hint: Saul claimed he kept the animals to sacrifice to God.

questions.question: What was David's first reaction after being rebuked by Nathan for his sin against Uriah and Bathsheba?
options: To plead with God to preserve his child's life
To request that punishments not come to pass
To ask Nathan to beg Bathsheba to pardon him
questions.explanation: After Nathan confronted David, David immediately confessed his sin (Psalm 51). When the child became ill, David fasted and pleaded with God for the child's life, showing his repentance.
questions.hint: After the child died, David worshiped God and moved on.

questions.question: 'If he (Baal) is a god, let him contend for himself because his altar has been pulled down.' Who pulled down the altar of Baal?
questions.explanation: Gideon, at God's command, tore down his father's altar of Baal and cut down the Asherah pole, then built an altar to the Lord and sacrificed on it.
questions.hint: The men of the town wanted to kill Gideon, but his father defended him.

questions.question: Why did Hannah pray to God at Shiloh for a son?
options: Elkanah hated her for being barren
She had sinned against the Lord
She wanted her son to succeed Eli
questions.explanation: Hannah was deeply distressed because her rival Peninnah (Elkanah's other wife) provoked her severely year after year about her barrenness, causing her to weep and not eat.
questions.hint: Hannah vowed to give her son to the Lord's service.

questions.question: Why did God pronounce utter destruction on the Amalekites?
options: They were hostile to the Kenites
They were hostile neighbors of Israel
They opposed Israel on the way from Egypt
questions.explanation: The Amalekites attacked the Israelites at Rephidim after the Exodus, striking the weak and weary, and God declared perpetual war against them as judgment.
questions.hint: Moses built an altar called 'The Lord is my Banner' after this battle.

questions.question: As David was about to die, he charged Solomon to keep God's statutes, commandments, ordinances, and testimonies for what purpose?
options: To prosper and preserve the Davidic dynasty
To have children to succeed him
To avenge the enemies of David
questions.topic: Old Testament - David's Charge to Solomon
questions.explanation: David instructed Solomon to keep God's commandments so that he might prosper in all he did and so that the Lord might establish His word concerning David's dynasty.
questions.hint: The conditional promise was that David's descendants would rule if they remained faithful.

questions.question: In the trade treaty between King Solomon and Hiram of Lebanon, Solomon was to supply wheat and oil while Hiram was to supply what?
questions.explanation: Hiram agreed to supply Solomon with cedar and cypress timber from Lebanon for building the Temple and palace in exchange for wheat and oil.
questions.hint: This alliance was mutually beneficial and peaceful.

questions.question: To whom was this addressed: 'Your father made our yoke heavy. Now therefore lighten the hard service of your father and his heavy yoke upon us and we will serve you'?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Division of the Kingdom
questions.explanation: The people of Israel made this request to Rehoboam, Solomon's son, asking him to lighten the heavy labor burden imposed by Solomon.
questions.hint: Rehoboam foolishly rejected the elders' advice and answered harshly.

questions.question: According to Genesis Chapter 1, on the 6th day, what did God create?
questions.explanation: On the sixth day, God created land animals, cattle, creeping things, and finally created man (male and female) in His own image.
questions.hint: Only after creating man did God say it was 'very good'.

questions.question: How long did the flood last while Noah was in the ark?
questions.explanation: The floodwaters prevailed upon the earth for 150 days (five months) before the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
questions.hint: Noah was in the ark for over a year total.

questions.question: 'Walk before me, and be blameless.' How old was the addressee when this voice came?
questions.explanation: God appeared to Abram (later Abraham) when he was 99 years old, reaffirming the covenant and changing his name from Abram to Abraham.
questions.hint: This was 13 years after Ishmael was born.

questions.question: Why did Jacob say to his children, 'You would bring down my grey hairs with sorrow to Sheol'?
options: He feared Benjamin would be lost like Joseph
questions.topic: Old Testament - Jacob and His Sons
questions.explanation: Jacob refused to send Benjamin to Egypt, fearing that harm would come to him as he believed had happened to Joseph, which would cause him to die of grief.
questions.hint: Reuben offered his own two sons as surety for Benjamin's safety.

questions.question: When the magicians said to Pharaoh, 'This is the finger of God,' after which plague was this statement made?
questions.explanation: When Aaron struck the dust and it became gnats (lice/kinnim) on man and beast, the magicians could not replicate this plague and declared it was the finger of God.
questions.hint: This was the third plague, after blood and frogs.

questions.question: The father of Moses was of which tribe of Israel?
questions.explanation: Moses' father, Amram, was of the tribe of Levi, the priestly tribe. His mother, Jochebed, was also a Levite.
questions.hint: Aaron and Miriam were also from the tribe of Levi.

questions.question: When did the Israelites say, 'All that the Lord has spoken we will do'?
options: When Moses spoke before his death
After the incident of the golden calf
questions.explanation: At Mount Sinai, after Moses reported God's words to the people, they all answered together, 'All that the Lord has spoken we will do.'
questions.hint: This was before the Ten Commandments were given.

questions.question: The 5th of the Ten Commandments is a warning about what?
options: Not to bow down to idols
To honour one's father and mother
questions.explanation: The fifth commandment is a positive command: 'Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land which the Lord your God gives you.'
questions.hint: This is the first commandment with a promise attached.

questions.question: Where did Moses say, 'Hear now, you rebels: shall we bring forth water for you out of this rock?'
questions.topic: Old Testament - Water from the Rock
questions.explanation: At Meribah in the wilderness of Zin, the people quarreled with Moses for water. Moses struck the rock twice instead of speaking to it as God commanded.
questions.hint: This disobedience cost Moses entry into the Promised Land.

questions.question: The spies sent into the land of Canaan were NOT required to find out whether the?
options: People who dwelt in it were few or many
Land they dwelt in was good or bad
questions.explanation: Moses instructed the spies to find out about the people (few/many), the land (good/bad), the cities (camps/strongholds), and whether the land was fertile, but not specifically about forests.
questions.hint: They were to bring back fruit of the land.

questions.question: Who was chosen to lead the Israelites after Moses' death?
questions.explanation: God commanded Moses to commission Joshua son of Nun as his successor, and Joshua was filled with the spirit of wisdom to lead the people into the Promised Land.
questions.hint: Joshua had been Moses' assistant since his youth.

questions.question: Where was Joshua's first victory?
questions.explanation: Joshua's first victory was at Jericho, where the walls fell down after the Israelites marched around the city for seven days and shouted.
questions.hint: Rahab and her family were spared.

questions.question: Who put Sisera to death?
questions.explanation: After Sisera fled from battle, Jael, wife of Heber the Kenite, drove a tent peg through his temple while he slept, fulfilling Deborah's prophecy that a woman would kill him.
questions.hint: Jael offered Sisera milk and covered him with a rug before killing him.

questions.question: 'But I will be with you and you will smite the Midianites as one man.' To whom was this said by God?
questions.explanation: The angel of the Lord appeared to Gideon and assured him of victory over the Midianites, despite Gideon feeling unworthy and weak.
questions.hint: Gideon asked for multiple signs, including the fleece test.

questions.question: What does the name 'Samuel' mean?
options: I have asked him of the Lord
questions.explanation: Hannah named her son Samuel, meaning 'heard of God' or 'asked of the Lord,' because she had prayed for him and God answered her request.
questions.hint: Samuel was dedicated to the Lord's service as a Nazirite.

questions.question: Why were Samuel's sons rejected as judges?
options: They were not from the royal family
They perverted justice and took bribes
questions.explanation: Samuel's sons, Joel and Abijah, did not walk in his ways; they turned aside after dishonest gain, took bribes, and perverted justice, leading Israel to demand a king.
questions.hint: This was the primary reason Israel asked for a king like other nations.

questions.question: Why did God take the kingdom from Saul?
options: He disobeyed the Lord by offering burnt sacrifices himself
questions.explanation: Saul disobeyed God by offering a burnt sacrifice himself instead of waiting for Samuel as instructed, and later by sparing King Agag and the Amalekite livestock.
questions.hint: Samuel told Saul, 'To obey is better than sacrifice.'

questions.question: How was Saul killed?
options: He took his own sword and fell upon it
questions.explanation: After being severely wounded by Philistine archers, Saul asked his armour-bearer to kill him. When the armour-bearer refused, Saul fell on his own sword and died.
questions.hint: An Amalekite later claimed to have killed Saul, but David executed him.

questions.question: Who was the commander of David's army?
questions.explanation: Joab son of Zeruiah was the commander of David's army throughout most of his reign, though he was often ruthless and politically ambitious.
questions.hint: Joab killed Abner and Absalom against David's wishes.

questions.question: With whom did King David commit adultery?
questions.explanation: David committed adultery with Bathsheba, the wife of Uriah the Hittite, while Uriah was away fighting in David's army.
questions.hint: This led David to arrange Uriah's death in battle.

questions.question: Which prophet encouraged Jeroboam I to revolt at the initial stage?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Division of the Kingdom
questions.explanation: The prophet Ahijah the Shilonite met Jeroboam and tore his new garment into twelve pieces, giving ten pieces to Jeroboam as a sign that God would give him ten tribes.
questions.hint: This was a prophetic act symbolizing the division of the kingdom.

questions.question: Who built the city of Samaria?
questions.explanation: Omri, the sixth king of the northern kingdom of Israel, bought the hill of Samaria from Shemer and built the city, making it the new capital of Israel.
questions.hint: Samaria remained the capital until the Assyrian conquest.

questions.question: The prophet from Tekoa was?
questions.explanation: Amos was a shepherd from Tekoa, a small town in Judah, whom God called to prophesy against the northern kingdom of Israel.
questions.hint: Amos was not a professional prophet but a herdsman and dresser of sycamore trees.

questions.question: Which king reigned after Hezekiah?
questions.explanation: Manasseh, son of Hezekiah, became king of Judah at age 12 and reigned for 55 years, doing great evil in the sight of the Lord.
questions.hint: Manasseh later repented in captivity, but the damage was done.

questions.question: Which of the following was NOT a complaint of the people when Nehemiah was rebuilding Jerusalem?
options: Let us get grain that we may eat
We are mortgaging our fields and houses
We have borrowed money for the king's tax
The strength of the burden-bearers is failing
questions.explanation: The people complained about food shortages, mortgaging property, and borrowing money to pay taxes, but 'the strength of the burden-bearers is failing' was not among the listed complaints.
questions.hint: Nehemiah responded by rebuking the nobles and officials for exacting usury.

questions.question: Why was Jesus said to be 'of Nazareth'?
options: His ancestor David lived in Nazareth
He was brought up in the city of Nazareth
He made Nazareth his preaching headquarters
questions.topic: New Testament - Early Life of Jesus
questions.explanation: After returning from Egypt, Joseph settled in Nazareth, a town in Galilee, where Jesus grew up, fulfilling the prophecy that He would be called a Nazarene.
questions.hint: Nazareth was a despised and insignificant town.

questions.question: Why did Luke give the most detailed account of Christ's infancy?
options: He was present at the birth
He was a physician interested in births
questions.explanation: Luke, a careful historian, likely interviewed Mary and other eyewitnesses to compile a detailed and orderly account of Jesus' birth and early life.
questions.hint: Luke's Gospel contains the annunciation, visitation, Magnificat, and the shepherds.

questions.question: 'You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the wrath to come?' was part of the preaching of whom?
questions.explanation: John the Baptist addressed these harsh words to the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to his baptism, calling them to genuine repentance, not mere ritual.
questions.hint: He warned that God could raise up children to Abraham from stones.

questions.question: Which of these statements about the temptation of Jesus is correct?
options: The order of temptations varies in Matthew and Luke
The temptation story is found in Mark only
Only Luke gives the temptation story
questions.explanation: Matthew and Luke record the same three temptations but in different order. Matthew ends with the kingdoms of the world; Luke ends with the pinnacle of the temple.
questions.hint: Mark mentions the temptation but gives no details.

questions.question: Which of these was NOT one of the twelve apostles?
questions.explanation: Luke was a physician and companion of Paul who wrote the Gospel of Luke and Acts, but he was not one of the twelve apostles.
questions.hint: The twelve included Peter, Andrew, James, John, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Zealot, and Judas Iscariot.

questions.question: Who said, 'I am not worthy to have you come under my roof'?
questions.topic: New Testament - Healing of Centurion's Servant
questions.explanation: The Roman centurion sent elders to Jesus, expressing his unworthiness and his faith that Jesus could heal his servant by simply speaking a word.
questions.hint: Jesus marveled at this Gentile's great faith, saying He had not found such faith in Israel.

questions.question: The parable 'The Kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field' teaches about what?
options: The hidden nature of the Kingdom
The cleverness of the land speculator
The urgency of entry into the Kingdom
questions.explanation: This parable teaches the incomparable value of the Kingdom of God, for which a person should be willing to sell all they have to obtain it.
questions.hint: The man sold everything he had in joy to buy the field.

questions.question: The renowned interpreters of the Mosaic Law in the time of Jesus were the?
questions.explanation: The scribes were professional interpreters and teachers of the Mosaic Law, often associated with the Pharisees in their opposition to Jesus.
questions.hint: Scribes were also called 'lawyers' or 'teachers of the law'.

questions.question: 'This is my beloved Son: listen to him' was said on what occasion?
questions.explanation: At the Transfiguration, a voice from the cloud declared, 'This is my beloved Son; listen to him,' affirming Jesus' divine sonship and authority.
questions.hint: This event occurred on a mountain with Peter, James, and John present.

questions.question: In the parable of the wheat and tares, who is the owner of the field?
questions.explanation: Jesus explained that the one who sows the good seed (the wheat) is the Son of Man, referring to Himself as the owner of the field (the world).
questions.hint: The field represents the world, and the good seeds are the sons of the Kingdom.

questions.question: When Herod the Tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus, who did he think Jesus was?
options: John the Baptist raised from the dead
questions.explanation: Herod Antipas, who had beheaded John the Baptist, was troubled and believed that Jesus was John the Baptist raised from the dead, now performing miracles.
questions.hint: Others thought Jesus was Elijah or one of the prophets.

questions.question: Who asked Jesus, 'Grant us to sit, one at your right hand and one at your left, in your glory'?
questions.topic: New Testament - Request of Zebedee's Sons
questions.explanation: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to Jesus with their mother to request the highest positions in His kingdom, revealing their misunderstanding of true greatness.
questions.hint: Jesus responded that such positions are not His to grant.

questions.question: 'Today salvation has come to this house, for this is also a son of Abraham.' This was said of whom?
questions.explanation: Jesus said this about Zacchaeus, the chief tax collector of Jericho, after he repented and promised to give half his goods to the poor and repay those he cheated fourfold.
questions.hint: Zacchaeus climbed a sycamore tree to see Jesus.

questions.question: In the parable of the ten maidens, why were the five called foolish?
options: They were sleeping before the marriage feast
They did not take additional oil with them
questions.explanation: The foolish maidens took their lamps but no extra oil, while the wise took flasks of oil. When the bridegroom was delayed, their lamps went out and they were unprepared.
questions.hint: This parable teaches readiness for Christ's return.

questions.question: 'I find no crime in this man.' Who made this statement about Jesus?
questions.explanation: Pontius Pilate, after examining Jesus, declared three times that he found no fault or crime in Him, yet he still handed Him over to be crucified.
questions.hint: Pilate washed his hands to symbolize his innocence.

questions.question: Concerning the events preceding the end of the world, what did Jesus ask His followers to pray would not happen?
questions.explanation: Jesus told His disciples to pray that their flight from the coming tribulation would not be in winter or on a Sabbath, as winter would make travel difficult and Sabbath restrictions would hinder escape.
questions.hint: This teaching is part of the Olivet Discourse.

questions.question: Who was Theophilus?
options: The one for whom Acts was written
questions.topic: New Testament - Gospel of Luke and Acts
questions.explanation: Theophilus was a person (likely a Gentile believer or Roman official) to whom Luke addressed both his Gospel and the Acts of the Apostles, providing an orderly account of Jesus' life and the early church.
questions.hint: His name means 'lover of God' or 'friend of God'.

questions.question: Who was told by an angel, 'Dress yourself and put on your sandals'?
questions.topic: New Testament - Peter's Escape from Prison
questions.explanation: An angel appeared to Peter while he was imprisoned by Herod, and the angel instructed him to dress, put on his sandals, and follow him out of the prison.
questions.hint: Peter thought he was seeing a vision until he was actually outside.

questions.question: On the road to Gaza, Philip converted and baptized whom?
questions.topic: New Testament - Philip and the Ethiopian
questions.explanation: Philip met an Ethiopian eunuch, a high official (minister) of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was reading Isaiah. Philip explained the scripture and baptized him.
questions.hint: The Spirit of the Lord then carried Philip away.

questions.question: After his conversion, where did Paul first go?
questions.explanation: After being blinded on the road to Damascus and then healed by Ananias, Paul remained in Damascus for some days, preaching in the synagogues that Jesus is the Son of God.
questions.hint: He later went to Arabia and then returned to Damascus before going to Jerusalem.

questions.question: An eloquent Jew from Alexandria, well versed in the Scriptures, was?
questions.explanation: Apollos was a learned Jewish man from Alexandria who knew the Scriptures well and taught accurately about Jesus, though he only knew the baptism of John. Priscilla and Aquila later instructed him more fully.
questions.hint: He became a powerful apologist for the gospel.

questions.question: The ecumenical Council of 49 A.D. held in Jerusalem was mainly to discuss what?
questions.explanation: The Jerusalem Council addressed whether Gentile converts needed to be circumcised and keep the Law of Moses for salvation. The decision was that salvation is by grace through faith, but Gentiles should abstain from idolatry, sexual immorality, and strangled meat with blood.
questions.hint: James, Peter, and Paul all spoke at this council.

questions.question: An altar was set up to the Unknown God in which Greek town?
questions.explanation: In Athens, Paul observed an altar with the inscription 'To the unknown god' and used it as an opportunity to proclaim the true God, who is not unknown.
questions.hint: Paul quoted Greek poets Aratus and Epimenides in his speech.

questions.question: 'You have appealed to Caesar: To Caesar then you shall go.' Who made this statement to Paul?
questions.topic: New Testament - Paul's Appeal to Caesar
questions.explanation: When Paul appealed to Caesar (a right of Roman citizens), Festus, the governor, declared that to Caesar he would go, thus sending Paul to Rome for trial.
questions.hint: This fulfilled God's plan for Paul to witness in Rome.

questions.question: Why did the people of Malta think that Paul must be a murderer?
options: He was bitten by a poisonous snake
He was being taken to Rome as a prisoner
questions.topic: New Testament - Paul Shipwrecked on Malta
questions.explanation: When a viper fastened onto Paul's hand as he gathered sticks for a fire, the islanders assumed he was a murderer whom justice had not allowed to live, but when he suffered no harm, they thought he was a god.
questions.hint: Paul then healed many on the island.

questions.question: 'This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman because she was ...' What completes this statement?
questions.explanation: Adam said this when God presented Eve to him, recognizing that she was taken from his side and therefore is bone of his bones and flesh of his flesh.
questions.hint: This establishes the divine institution of marriage.

questions.question: When God called Abram out of Haran, where was his first place of call in Canaan?
questions.explanation: Abram first came to the site of Shechem, to the oak of Moreh, where the Lord appeared to him and promised to give the land to his descendants.
questions.hint: There Abram built his first altar to the Lord in Canaan.

questions.question: 'This is none other than the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven.' Who said this and where?
questions.explanation: Jacob said this after dreaming of a ladder reaching to heaven with angels ascending and descending, realizing that God was in that place, which he named Bethel.
questions.hint: He set up the stone he had used as a pillow as a pillar.

questions.question: The Egyptians enslaved the Israelites and forced them to build what?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Israelite Slavery in Egypt
questions.explanation: The Egyptians set taskmasters over the Israelites to oppress them with forced labor, building the store-cities of Pithom and Raamses for Pharaoh.
questions.hint: This was part of God's prophecy to Abraham that his descendants would be strangers and enslaved for 400 years.

questions.question: The Passover was to remind the Israelites of what?
options: God's execution of judgment on Egypt's gods
God's smiting of all the firstborn in Egypt
The seven days of eating unleavened bread
The eating of roasted lamb while standing
questions.explanation: The Passover commemorated God's judgment on the gods of Egypt when the angel of death passed over the houses marked with lamb's blood, sparing the firstborn of Israel while striking Egypt's firstborn and their gods.
questions.hint: Exodus 12:12 says, 'On all the gods of Egypt I will execute judgments.'

questions.question: The consecration of the people of Israel took place at the foot of which mountain?
questions.explanation: At Mount Sinai, God instructed Moses to consecrate the people, have them wash their garments, and prepare to meet Him on the third day when He would descend upon the mountain.
questions.hint: The mountain was off-limits; anyone who touched it would be put to death.

questions.question: 'It is not the sound of shouting for victory, or the sound of the cry of defeat, but the sound of singing that I hear.' What occasion is reflected in this statement?
options: Rejoicing for water from the rock
Praising God after receiving the Ten Commandments
The apostasy of Aaron and the people
questions.explanation: Joshua heard the sound of the people singing and shouting as they worshiped the golden calf that Aaron had made, while Moses was descending Mount Sinai with the tablets.
questions.hint: Moses broke the tablets when he saw the calf and the dancing.

questions.question: When Moses was instructed to view the Promised Land from afar, what did he do?
options: Summoned the elders and informed them
Prayed for a successor to shepherd the people
questions.explanation: After God showed Moses the Promised Land from Mount Nebo, Moses prayed for God to appoint a successor to lead the people, and God appointed Joshua.
questions.hint: Moses died at age 120, and God buried him in an unknown location.

questions.question: Who gave this advice: 'Now therefore, fear the Lord and serve him in sincerity and faithfulness; put away the gods which your fathers served beyond the River and in Egypt, and serve the Lord'?
questions.explanation: Joshua gave this farewell address to all Israel at Shechem, challenging them to choose whom they would serve, while declaring, 'As for me and my house, we will serve the Lord.'
questions.hint: The people responded that they would also serve the Lord.

questions.question: 'Rule over us you and your son and your grandson also, for you have delivered us out of the hand of Midian.' To whom was this addressed?
questions.explanation: After Gideon delivered Israel from the Midianites, the people asked him to rule over them and establish a dynasty, but Gideon refused, saying the Lord would rule over them.
questions.hint: Gideon did, however, make an ephod that became a snare.

questions.question: Who asked, 'Which is better for you that all seventy of the sons of Jerubbaal rule over you, or that one rule over you?'
questions.explanation: Jotham, the only surviving son of Gideon (Jerubbaal) after Abimelech killed his 70 brothers, spoke this parable from Mount Gerizim, comparing Abimelech to a bramble.
questions.hint: The parable of the trees choosing a king.

questions.question: At childhood, why did the word of the Lord come to Samuel?
options: He had the qualities of a prophet
He was to announce punishment for Eli's house
questions.explanation: God called Samuel to deliver a message of judgment against Eli's house because Eli's sons were blasphemous and Eli failed to restrain them.
questions.hint: Samuel was reluctant to tell Eli the vision, but Eli insisted.

questions.question: Saul said, 'Cursed be the man who takes food before evening before I am able to avenge myself on my enemies.' During which battle was this said?
questions.explanation: During the battle against the Philistines at Michmash, Saul made a foolish oath cursing any soldier who ate food before evening, which caused the people to be faint and sin by eating meat with blood.
questions.hint: Jonathan, who had not heard the oath, ate honey and was almost put to death.

questions.question: Who touched the Ark and died when David was returning it to Jerusalem?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Uzzah and the Ark
questions.explanation: When the oxen stumbled, Uzzah put out his hand to steady the Ark of God. The Lord struck him dead for his irreverence, as only priests were permitted to touch the Ark.
questions.hint: David was angry and afraid of the Lord that day.

questions.question: What suggestion did Nathan make to Bathsheba to foil Adonijah's attempt to usurp the throne?
options: Remind David of his promise that Solomon would succeed him
Have Solomon secretly anointed by Zadok
Plead with Adonijah to abandon the attempt
questions.explanation: Nathan advised Bathsheba to go to King David and remind him of his oath that Solomon would be king, and then Nathan would come and confirm her words, securing Solomon's succession.
questions.hint: David then immediately had Solomon anointed king.

questions.question: Why was it wrong for King Solomon to have married foreign women?
options: They would entice him to worship idols
The women of Israel were more beautiful
A king should not have too many children
questions.explanation: Solomon's foreign wives turned his heart after other gods, leading him to build high places for Chemosh and Molech, which was evil in the sight of the Lord.
questions.hint: This directly violated God's command for kings not to multiply wives.

questions.question: How many months were the Israelites on forced labor in Lebanon allowed to spend at home for every month at the labor camp?
questions.explanation: Solomon conscripted 30,000 men from Israel to work in Lebanon, sending them in rotations of 10,000 per month so that they spent one month in Lebanon and two months at home.
questions.hint: This system was used to gather timber for the Temple.

questions.question: Which king said, 'Behold your gods, O Israel, who brought you up out of the land of Egypt'?
questions.explanation: King Jeroboam of Israel set up two golden calves in Bethel and Dan, telling the people it was too much to go to Jerusalem to worship, thus leading Israel into idolatry.
questions.hint: This became the sin of Jeroboam that plagued the Northern Kingdom.

questions.question: The jar of meal shall not be spent and the cruise of oil shall not fail, until the day that the Lord sends rain upon the earth. Which statement about this is UNTRUE?
options: The meal and oil were continually replenished
The widow of Zarephath prepared food for Elijah
The widow and her son ate and waited for death
The woman was gathering sticks for her last meal
questions.topic: Old Testament - Elijah and the Widow
questions.explanation: The statement that 'the widow and her son ate and waited for their death' is untrue because God miraculously provided for them daily, and they did not die of starvation.
questions.hint: Elijah stayed with the widow during the drought.

questions.question: Which king did not assassinate his predecessor but was assassinated by his successor?
questions.explanation: Shallum assassinated Zechariah and reigned for one month before being assassinated by Menahem, making him a king who did not assassinate his predecessor but was himself assassinated.
questions.hint: This was during the chaotic period of Israel's final years.

questions.question: Hosea said that the children of Israel shall dwell many days without king or prince, without sacrifice or pillar, without what?
questions.explanation: Hosea prophesied that Israel would live many days without king, prince, sacrifice, pillar, ephod, or teraphim (household idols), indicating a complete absence of religious and political institutions.
questions.hint: This was fulfilled during the Assyrian exile.

questions.question: Who burned his son as an offering, practiced soothsaying and augury, and dealt with mediums and wizards?
questions.explanation: Manasseh, king of Judah, did much evil in the sight of the Lord, including sacrificing his son, practicing witchcraft, and consulting mediums, leading to God's judgment on Judah.
questions.hint: He later repented while imprisoned in Babylon.

questions.question: Which Egyptian king made Eliakim king of Judah and changed his name to Jehoiakim?
questions.explanation: Pharaoh Neco of Egypt deposed Jehoahaz and made Eliakim (Jehoiakim) king of Judah as a vassal, imposing a heavy tribute on the land.
questions.hint: This occurred after King Josiah was killed at Megiddo.

questions.question: What did Jeremiah write to the exiles in Babylon to encourage them to do?
options: Come back home to rebuild the temple
questions.topic: Old Testament - Jeremiah's Letter to Exiles
questions.explanation: Jeremiah wrote to the exiles, instructing them to build houses, plant gardens, marry, and seek the welfare of the city where they were exiled, for in its welfare they would find their welfare.
questions.hint: He told them the exile would last 70 years.

questions.question: Which prophet made this prophecy: 'The ransomed of the Lord shall return, and come to Zion with singing; everlasting joy shall be upon their heads'?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Isaiah's Prophecy of Restoration
questions.explanation: Isaiah prophesied the joyful return of the exiles to Zion, promising that everlasting joy would crown their heads as sorrow and sighing fled away.
questions.hint: This is part of Isaiah's 'Servant Songs' and messages of comfort.

questions.question: What specific reply did John the Baptist give to the soldiers during his preaching?
options: Collect no more than is appointed you
Do not begin to say: we have Abraham as our Father
questions.topic: New Testament - John the Baptist's Preaching
questions.explanation: When soldiers asked John what they should do, he replied, 'Rob no one by violence or by false accusation, and be content with your wages.'
questions.hint: He gave different answers to different groups (crowds, tax collectors, soldiers).

questions.question: The order of the locations of Jesus' temptations according to Matthew is?
options: Wilderness - high mountain - pinnacle of the Temple
Wilderness - pinnacle of the Temple - high mountain
High mountain - wilderness - pinnacle of the Temple
questions.explanation: Matthew's order is: 1) Wilderness (turn stones to bread), 2) Pinnacle of the Temple (throw yourself down), 3) High mountain (kingdoms of the world).
questions.hint: Luke's order differs, ending with the pinnacle of the Temple.

questions.question: When Christ called Levi (Matthew) to follow Him, what did Levi do?
options: Accounted for the tax he collected
questions.explanation: When Jesus said 'Follow me,' Levi (Matthew) rose, left everything behind, including his tax booth and wealth, and followed Jesus immediately.
questions.hint: He then hosted a great feast for Jesus at his house.

questions.question: Which miracle touched on the theme of ingratitude?
questions.topic: New Testament - Healing of the Ten Lepers
questions.explanation: Of the ten lepers Jesus healed, only one (a Samaritan) returned to give thanks, highlighting the ingratitude of the other nine.
questions.hint: Jesus asked, 'Were not ten cleansed? Where are the nine?'

questions.question: The parable of the unmerciful servant teaches that we should what?
options: Exercise patience until our debtors pay
Forgive our debtors from our hearts
questions.explanation: The parable teaches that because God has forgiven us an immeasurable debt, we must forgive others from the heart, not just outwardly.
questions.hint: The unforgiving servant was handed over to the torturers.

questions.question: The ways in which various people hear the word of God are described in which parable?
questions.topic: New Testament - Parable of the Sower
questions.explanation: The Parable of the Sower describes four types of soil representing different responses to God's word: the path (unresponsive), rocky ground (shallow), thorns (distracted), and good soil (fruitful).
questions.hint: Jesus explained this parable to His disciples privately.

questions.question: 'But if it is by the Spirit of God that I cast out demons, then what?'
options: The kingdom of Satan cannot stand
The kingdom of God has come upon you
The kingdom of Satan is already divided
questions.explanation: Jesus argued that if He casts out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon His hearers, demonstrating God's present reign.
questions.hint: This was Jesus' response to accusations that He cast out demons by Beelzebul.

questions.question: Jesus taught that fasting in addition to prayers is necessary in some situations, such as during the healing of whom?
questions.topic: New Testament - Healing of the Epileptic Boy
questions.explanation: After the disciples could not cast out the demon from the epileptic boy, Jesus told them that this kind of demon only comes out through prayer and fasting.
questions.hint: The boy's father cried, 'I believe; help my unbelief!'

questions.question: Why did Jesus not grant the request of the sons of Zebedee?
options: They were not qualified for the privilege
He did not want to encourage rivalry
It was not His to grant
They could not drink with Jesus' cup
questions.topic: New Testament - Request of James and John
questions.explanation: Jesus told James and John that to sit at His right or left hand was not His to grant, but belongs to those for whom it has been prepared by the Father.
questions.hint: He also asked if they could drink the cup He would drink.

questions.question: The parable which teaches persistence in prayer is that of the?
questions.topic: New Testament - Parable of the Persistent Widow
questions.explanation: The parable of the persistent widow and the unjust judge teaches that if an unjust judge will grant justice through persistent pleading, God will surely answer His elect who cry to Him day and night.
questions.hint: Jesus told this parable to encourage prayer and not lose heart.

questions.question: To give the widow's mite means to give?
questions.explanation: The widow gave two small copper coins, which was all she had to live on. Jesus commended her because she gave everything, while the rich gave out of their abundance.
questions.hint: Her gift was worth more in God's eyes than large sums from the rich.

questions.question: Which of these women was absent during the crucifixion of Jesus Christ?
questions.explanation: Elizabeth, the mother of John the Baptist, is not recorded as being present at the crucifixion. The Gospels mention Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of Jesus, and Salome among others.
questions.hint: Elizabeth's last appearance in Scripture is at the birth of John the Baptist.

questions.question: To explain the outpouring of the Holy Spirit on the day of Pentecost, Peter quoted which prophet?
questions.explanation: Peter quoted the prophet Joel (Joel 2:28-32) to explain the outpouring of the Holy Spirit, saying 'In the last days, God says, I will pour out my Spirit on all people.'
questions.hint: This prophecy included sons and daughters prophesying, young men seeing visions, and old men dreaming dreams.

questions.question: To whom was this addressed: 'While it remained unsold, did it not remain your own? And after it was sold, was it not at your disposal? How is it that you have contrived this deed in your heart? You have not lied to men but to God.'
questions.explanation: Peter spoke these words to Ananias, who had lied about the proceeds from the sale of his property, pretending to give all while keeping back part for himself.
questions.hint: Ananias fell down dead immediately after hearing these words.

questions.question: 'Truly I perceive that God shows no partiality.' Who said this?
questions.explanation: Peter said this after seeing the Holy Spirit poured out on Gentiles (Cornelius and his household), realizing that God accepts people from every nation who fear Him and do right.
questions.hint: This led to the baptism of the first Gentile converts.

questions.question: 'Our sister, be the mother of thousands and ten thousands; and may your descendants possess the gate of those who hate them.' Who was the sister referred to?
questions.explanation: Rebekah's family blessed her with these words as she left to marry Isaac, expressing their hope that she would become the mother of countless descendants.
questions.hint: This blessing was fulfilled through Jacob and Esau becoming two nations.

questions.question: 'Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not be heir with my son Isaac.' Who was the slave woman?
questions.explanation: Sarah demanded that Abraham cast out Hagar the Egyptian slave woman and her son Ishmael, as Ishmael was mocking Isaac on the day of his weaning.
questions.hint: God told Abraham to listen to Sarah, promising to make a nation of Ishmael as well.

questions.question: 'Thorns and thistles it shall bring forth to you and you shall eat the plants of the field. In the sweat of your face you shall eat bread.' To whom were these words spoken?
questions.explanation: God pronounced this curse on Adam after he ate the forbidden fruit, declaring that the ground would be cursed and that man would now labor painfully for food.
questions.hint: This was part of the consequences of the Fall.

questions.question: Why did Abraham circumcise his son Isaac on the eighth day?
options: It was the custom of his tribe
God commanded him to do so
He wanted to distinguish Isaac from Ishmael
Isaac was the son of promise
questions.explanation: God commanded Abraham that every male child was to be circumcised on the eighth day as a sign of the covenant. Abraham obeyed this command with Isaac.
questions.hint: This command was given when Abraham was 99 years old.

questions.question: Which of Joseph's brothers was bound by Joseph when they went to Egypt to buy grain?
questions.topic: Old Testament - Joseph and His Brothers
questions.explanation: Joseph bound Simeon before his brothers and kept him as a hostage while the others returned to Canaan to bring Benjamin back to Egypt.
questions.hint: Simeon was the second son of Jacob by Leah.

questions.question: Where was Joshua buried?
questions.explanation: Joshua was buried in the hill-country of Ephraim, north of Mount Gaash, in the territory of his inheritance.
questions.hint: Timnath-serah in the hill-country of Ephraim was the city given to Joshua.

questions.question: When Moses threw ashes from the kiln toward heaven in the sight of Pharaoh, what resulted?
options: Boils and sores on man and beast
questions.explanation: Moses threw ashes from the kiln toward heaven, and they became fine dust that caused boils breaking out in sores on man and beast throughout Egypt.
questions.hint: This was the sixth plague, and the magicians could not stand before Moses.

questions.question: Whoever touches the 'mountain of God' shall be put to death by what means?
questions.explanation: God instructed Moses that anyone who touched Mount Sinai, whether man or beast, was to be stoned or shot with arrows, and not to be touched by hand.
questions.hint: The mountain was consecrated by God's presence.

questions.question: What did Aaron use the gold rings from the people's ears to make?
questions.explanation: Aaron took the gold rings from the ears of the people and fashioned them into a molten calf, which the Israelites then worshiped as the god who brought them out of Egypt.
questions.hint: This act of idolatry provoked God's anger.

questions.question: God commanded that Aaron should speak for Moses to the Israelites, but Moses should be to Aaron as what?
questions.explanation: God told Moses that Aaron would be his spokesman to the people, but Moses would be 'as God' to Aaron, meaning Aaron would receive divine messages from Moses to speak to the people.
questions.hint: This established Moses as God's direct representative.

questions.question: On the first day when the Israelites left part of the bread, it bred worms and became foul, but on the sixth day when they left part it did not become foul. Why?
options: God commanded them to keep it
Moses wanted to save them trouble
They had discovered a better way of preserving it
questions.explanation: On the sixth day, the Israelites were to gather twice as much manna and prepare it, keeping the leftovers for the Sabbath because God commanded that no work be done on the Sabbath, and the manna did not spoil.
questions.hint: This taught Israel to observe the Sabbath rest.

questions.question: Who was regarded as the greatest prophet in the Old Testament for seeing God face to face?
questions.explanation: Moses was unique among Old Testament prophets because the Lord spoke to him face to face, as a man speaks to his friend, unlike other prophets who received visions and dreams.
questions.hint: Deuteronomy 34:10 says no prophet has arisen in Israel like Moses.

questions.question: 'Whoever rebels against your commandment and disobeys your words, whatever you command him, shall be put to death: only be strong and of good courage.' To whom was this addressed?
questions.explanation: The people of Israel spoke these words to Joshua after Moses' death, affirming his leadership and pledging obedience, just as they had obeyed Moses.
questions.hint: This was part of the transition of leadership from Moses to Joshua.

questions.question: Which of these statements did God make to Joshua before the crossing of the Jordan?
options: You shall lead my people with the staff of Moses
The people of Israel will murmur against you
This book of the law shall not depart out of your mouth
You shall select men of valour to cross with you
questions.explanation: God commanded Joshua to meditate on the Book of the Law day and night and not let it depart from his mouth, so that he might be careful to do all that was written in it.
questions.hint: This promise came with the assurance of success and prosperity.

questions.question: Achan, who took of the devoted things during the fall of Jericho, was from which tribe?
questions.explanation: Achan was from the tribe of Judah. He took a Babylonian garment, 200 shekels of silver, and a bar of gold from Jericho, causing Israel's defeat at Ai.
questions.hint: He was stoned in the Valley of Achor.

questions.question: Who among the following was connected with the war between Israel and King Jabin of Canaan?
questions.explanation: Jael, the wife of Heber the Kenite, killed Sisera, the commander of King Jabin's army, by driving a tent peg through his temple while he slept.
questions.hint: Deborah prophesied that a woman would defeat Sisera.

questions.question: 'Alas, my daughter: you have brought me very low and you have become the cause of great trouble to me for I have opened my mouth to the Lord, and I cannot take back my vow.' Who said this?
questions.explanation: Jephthah made a foolish vow to sacrifice whatever came out of his house to meet him if God gave him victory over the Ammonites. His only daughter came out, and he mourned his vow.
questions.hint: His daughter asked for two months to mourn her virginity.

questions.question: How often did Elkanah go to worship and sacrifice to the Lord at Shiloh?
questions.explanation: Elkanah went up from his city annually to worship and sacrifice to the Lord of hosts at Shiloh, where the sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were priests.
questions.hint: It was during one of these annual visits that Hannah prayed for a son.

questions.question: Why did Uriah not wish to go down to his house?
options: Joab would set him in the forefront of battle
The Ark was in the battlefield
David had lain with his wife
questions.explanation: Uriah refused to go home to his wife while the Ark of God and the army of Israel were camped in the open field, showing his loyalty and honor, which contrasted with David's sin.
questions.hint: David's attempt to cover his sin failed because of Uriah's integrity.

questions.question: Complete: 'Then the spirit of the Lord will come mightily upon you, and you shall prophesy with them...' This was said to whom?
questions.explanation: Samuel spoke these words to Saul when he anointed him as the first king of Israel, telling him the signs that would confirm God's choice.
questions.hint: Saul did indeed prophesy among the prophets, fulfilling the sign.

questions.question: When did the evil spirit that tormented Saul leave him?
questions.explanation: Whenever the evil spirit from God tormented Saul, David would play the lyre, and Saul would be relieved and feel better, and the evil spirit would depart from him.
questions.hint: This led to David being brought into Saul's service.

questions.question: Where was Saul made king publicly before the Lord and before all the people of Israel?
questions.explanation: Samuel gathered the people at Mizpah, where lots were cast, and Saul was publicly proclaimed king before the Lord and all the people.
questions.hint: Saul had hidden himself among the baggage.

questions.question: When the Queen of Sheba heard Solomon's fame concerning the name of the Lord, what did she come to test him with?
questions.explanation: The Queen of Sheba came to Jerusalem to test Solomon with hard questions, and Solomon answered all her questions, demonstrating the wisdom God had given him.
questions.hint: She was overwhelmed by what she saw and heard.

questions.question: What was the greatest of David's domestic problems that seemed to break his heart?
options: The rape of Tamar by Amnon
The killing of Amnon by Absalom
The conspiracy of Absalom against him
The murder of Absalom by Joab
questions.explanation: The greatest heartbreak for David was his son Absalom's conspiracy to steal the kingdom from him, which forced David to flee Jerusalem and nearly cost him his throne and life.
questions.hint: David wept bitterly when he learned of Absalom's death despite the rebellion.

questions.question: Besides the gifts of wisdom, what other gift did the Lord promise to Solomon but with a condition?
questions.explanation: God promised Solomon riches and honor, and also long life if he would walk in God's ways and keep His statutes and commandments, as David his father had done.
questions.hint: This conditional promise is found in 1 Kings 3:14.

questions.question: Why did Joseph decide to divorce Mary quietly when he discovered she was with child?
options: Mary was too tender and lovely
The angel warned Joseph not to be harsh
questions.explanation: Joseph was a righteous (just) man and did not want to expose Mary to public disgrace, so he planned to divorce her quietly. An angel then revealed the divine nature of the conception.
questions.hint: The angel told Joseph to take Mary as his wife.

questions.question: Though Herod wanted to put John the Baptist to death, he feared the people because they held him to be a what?
questions.topic: New Testament - Death of John the Baptist
questions.explanation: Herod feared the multitude because they regarded John as a prophet, so he initially protected John, though he eventually had him beheaded to please Herodias and her daughter.
questions.hint: John had rebuked Herod for taking his brother Philip's wife.

questions.question: Which of these was NOT included in the instructions given to the disciples during their mission?
questions.topic: New Testament - Commissioning of the Twelve
questions.explanation: Jesus instructed the disciples to heal the sick, cleanse lepers, raise the dead, and cast out demons, but He never told them to judge the people.
questions.hint: He also told them to take no money or extra clothing.

questions.question: When did Jesus ask His disciples not to disclose their vision of the transfiguration?
questions.explanation: Jesus commanded Peter, James, and John not to tell anyone what they had seen on the mount of transfiguration until the Son of Man had risen from the dead.
questions.hint: They kept the matter to themselves, questioning what 'rising from the dead' meant.

questions.question: When Peter confessed Jesus as the Christ, Jesus replied that it had been revealed to Peter by whom?
options: The Spirit of the living God
questions.explanation: Jesus told Peter, 'Blessed are you, Simon Bar-Jonah! For flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.'
questions.hint: This confession took place at Caesarea Philippi.

questions.question: Which of the following prophesied the signs of the end?
questions.explanation: Daniel prophesied about the end times, including the rise and fall of kingdoms, the coming of the Messiah, and the final resurrection (Daniel 7-12).
questions.hint: Jesus referred to 'the abomination of desolation spoken of by Daniel the prophet.'

questions.question: The parable of the labourers in the vineyard teaches above all about God's what?
questions.explanation: The parable teaches God's generosity and grace, as the landowner paid all workers the same wage regardless of how long they worked, representing that salvation is God's gift, not earned by works.
questions.hint: The workers who labored all day grumbled against the landowner's generosity.

questions.question: According to Jesus, every kingdom divided against itself is what?
questions.topic: New Testament - Jesus' Teaching on Division
questions.explanation: Jesus taught that a kingdom divided against itself is laid waste (brought to desolation), and a house divided against itself cannot stand, in response to accusations that He cast out demons by Beelzebul.
questions.hint: He used this argument to show the absurdity of Satan casting out Satan.

questions.question: Jesus taught that one should make friends quickly with one's whom?
questions.topic: New Testament - Sermon on the Mount
questions.explanation: Jesus taught to make friends quickly with your accuser while on the way to court, lest you be handed over to the judge and thrown into prison.
questions.hint: This teaching emphasizes reconciliation and avoiding judgment.

questions.question: Why did the Pharisees and Scribes accuse the disciples of Jesus of transgressing the tradition of the elders?
options: They did not regard synagogue rules
They did not pray before meals
questions.explanation: The Pharisees and scribes saw that some of Jesus' disciples ate bread with unwashed (ceremonially unclean) hands, which violated the tradition of the elders, not God's law.
questions.hint: Jesus responded by quoting Isaiah: 'This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.'

questions.question: 'Why this waste? For this ointment might have been sold for a large sum and given to the poor.' In which town did this event take place?
questions.explanation: This event took place in Bethany, at the house of Simon the leper, where Mary anointed Jesus' feet with expensive ointment, and Judas Iscariot objected.
questions.hint: Jesus defended Mary, saying she had anointed Him for His burial.

questions.question: At His triumphal entry into Jerusalem, why did Christ sit on an ass?
options: He wanted to fulfill the scriptures
questions.explanation: Jesus rode on a donkey to fulfill the prophecy of Zechariah 9:9: 'Behold, your king is coming to you, humble, and mounted on a donkey, on a colt, the foal of a donkey.'
questions.hint: This act demonstrated His messianic identity and humility.
